sections.subtitle: Current Form
sections.text: Genoa are struggling, having lost their last 3 matches and failing to keep a clean sheet in their last 11 games, as seen in their recent 3-1 loss to Roma and 4-0 defeat to Atalanta. Pisa, on the other hand, are winless in their last 6 matches, with a recent 0-2 home loss to Juventus and a 2-2 draw against Cagliari, showing some resilience but lacking defensive solidity.

sections.subtitle: Head-to-Head Record
sections.text: In their last two encounters, Genoa have a slight edge with a 1-0 win in 2022 and a 0-0 draw in 2023, indicating tight contests. Genoa dominated possession and created more chances in both games, but Pisa managed to hold firm defensively, especially in the draw.

sections.subtitle: Season Statistics
sections.text: Genoa, sitting 17th with 14 points, have scored 17 goals but conceded 27, showing defensive vulnerabilities. Pisa, in 19th with 11 points, have struggled offensively with just 12 goals while conceding 24, highlighting their difficulty in both attack and defense.

sections.subtitle: Key Players to Watch
sections.text: Genoa's Leo Østigård has been a threat with 3 goals and a 6.93 average rating, while Aarón Martín’s crossing ability (40 key passes, 3rd in the league) could be crucial. For Pisa, Stefano Moreo (3 goals) and M'Bala Nzola (3 goals) are key in attack, though their overall output remains limited.

sections.subtitle: Match Outlook
sections.text: With Sofascore predicting a 44% chance of a Genoa win, 28% draw, and 28% Pisa win, the home side appears favored despite recent poor form. Genoa’s home advantage at Luigi Ferraris and slightly better attacking stats may edge them ahead, but Pisa’s ability to grind out draws could make it a close game.
